question: [File: 'src/ratifiers/interfaces/ISetterRatifier.sol -> Scope: High Access-control failure in fee setter, role setter, tick-spacing setter, fee claimer, market parameter setter, or authorization setter'] [Function: SetterRatifier.setIsRootRatified + Midnight.isAuthorized (stale)] Can an authorized operator ratify a root via setIsRootRatified(maker, root, true), then the maker revokes the operator's authorization via setIsAuthorized(operator, false, maker), but the root remains ratified in SetterRatifier.isRootRatified[maker][root], under the precondition that revocation does not automatically unratify roots, causing takers to continue filling offers under the ratified root even after the maker intended to revoke all operator permissions, violating the invariant that gates and ratifiers must not be bypassable, causing scoped impact: stale ratification enables continued offer fills after intended revocation? Proof idea: ratify root, revoke operator, assert isRootRatified[maker][root] is still true and take() still succeeds.
